title: Microstylis calycina
intro: Microstylis calycina, also known as the 'Yellow Vexillary Orchid', is a type of terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Microstylis calycina include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.
taxonomy: Microstylis calycina is classified under the plant kingdom. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant species is also known as the 'Slender Tongue Orchid' and is further classified as a genus within the subfamily Orchidoideae. Microstylis calycina is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia. The specific classification of the plant is Microstylis calycina, within the order Asparagales.
